title: Tokens & Tokenization
description: Learn what tokens are, how they're created, and why they matter for language models
prerequisite_warning: None — this is a foundational module

learning_outcomes.subject: token
learning_outcomes.full_statement: Define what a token is in the context of natural language processing
learning_outcomes.description: Student can explain what a token is as a discrete unit of text that language models process

learning_outcomes.subject: token counting heuristic
learning_outcomes.context: to estimate token count from word count
learning_outcomes.full_statement: Apply the heuristic that 1 token ≈ 0.75 words to estimate token count in text
learning_outcomes.description: Student can estimate approximate token count given text length using the conversion ratio

learning_outcomes.subject: tokenization process
learning_outcomes.context: how tokenizers convert text into tokens
learning_outcomes.full_statement: Explain how tokenization works and why different tokenizers produce different outputs
learning_outcomes.description: Student can describe the process of breaking text into tokens and explain differences between tokenizer types

learning_outcomes.subject: importance of tokenization
learning_outcomes.context: for understanding model context windows and input truncation
learning_outcomes.full_statement: Explain why tokenization matters for predicting model behavior and avoiding truncation
learning_outcomes.description: Student can articulate the relationship between token count and context window limits
